findings.desc: unused imports Any,Sequence in typing block (grid.py:33,39)
upload validation diverges: validates expanded(label) layout so dep checks fire on upload; oracle validated trimmed layout (deps no-op) (grid.py:416-426)
tag protein_id resolved scan-only (last-wins); multi-proteoform-per-scan shows 0 tags for other proteoform vs oracle showing all scan tags (schema.py:383,390-397)
_explode_list_cols lacks null-row guard -> phantom null row for empty/zero-mass cells (schema.py:54-73)
tag_resolution mapping unused; only build_proteoform_scan_map used (spec-vs-oracle nuance) (schema.py:374-398)
Mass->3D drift: mass_table sets mass=mass_id(global) but 3D reads mass=mass_in_scan(per-scan ordinal); coincide only scan 0 (render.py:124,153)
Spectrum->3D wrong identity: deconv/anno spectrum set mass=peak_id(global) overloading the mass_in_scan slot (render.py:131,138,145)
initial-render divergence: auto-selects first experiment vs oracle blank-until-pick (FLASHDeconvViewer.py:54)
SequenceView peaks frame uses MonoMass; component hard-requires column 'mass' -> observedMasses=[] (also deconv) (render.py:62-69; schema.py:212-222)
scan->protein peak remap missing: filters protein_id on deconv_spectrum_tidy which lacks it -> peaks unfiltered (render.py:62-75; schema.py:403-447)
tagger overlay dead: tag_table emits scalar tag_id but tagger needs opaque TagData dict (render.py:199-203,141-149)
combined_spectrum blank: nothing sets scan/spectrum in TnT layout; protein/tag set protein/tag (render.py:141-149; FLASHTnTViewer.py:13-18)

findings.desc: precursor_signals 3D x-axis: oracle plots mass=mz*charge (get3DplotInputFromSNRPeaks x=peaks[1]*peaks[3], axis 'Mass') but schema has only mz/charge and render uses x_column=mz -> raw m/z on a 'Mass' axis; charge states collapse to m/z (schema.py _build_precursor_signals; render.py 3D_SN_plot)

LayoutManager upload of JSON with a wholly-empty experiment [] wipes the layout: expand() drops empty experiments, len(layout)<num_experiments triggers _reset_to_default; oracle inline-expand keeps empty exps (grid.py _handle_setting_buttons). Unreachable via app download/re-upload (trim drops empties)

axis titles fall back to column names: deconv_spectrum (mass/SumIntensity) + anno_spectrum (mz/intensity) + 4 heatmaps (rt/mass) lack x_label/y_label; oracle shows Monoisotopic Mass/m/z/Intensity/Retention Time (PlotlyLineplot.vue, PlotlyHeatmap.vue)

3D Precursor Signals: optional_filters=[mass] shows ALL the scan's peaks when no mass selected, but the oracle getPrecursorSignal renders EMPTY when no mass (precursor-scan find fails on scan-filtered data); round-2 3-builders-003 misread update.py. Revert optional_filters -> mass required -> empty until selected (oracle+spec parity)

quant 3D uses Plot3D default stem=True -> disconnected vertical spikes per point; oracle FLASHQuantView draws ONE connected mode:lines elution ridge per charge (z bracketed by -1000 sentinels). Pass stem=False (stem_baseline=-1000)

tagger leaks managed config keys (incl. absolute tag_data_path) into Vue args: lineplot.py _get_component_args_tagger copies self._config without the _MANAGED_CONFIG_KEYS guard the default path uses; benign (Vue ignores snake_case) but exposes a fs path. Fix in OpenMS-Insight lineplot.py
test_render_schema.py is_signal comment says peaks 0,1,2 are signal / idx3 not; actual SignalPeaks.peak_index is {0,1,3} (assertion sum==4 is correct, only the comment is wrong)

findings.desc: quant 3D: round-6 stem=False draws one continuous polyline per charge; oracle breaks isotope sub-traces WITHIN a charge (-1000 z-sentinels) -> spurious diagonal for multi-isotope features. Needs Plot3D series_column sub-trace breaks
quant 3D legend shows bare category '2' vs oracle 'Charge: 2' (FLASHQuantView name template). Needs Plot3D category_name_template

all 5 tables show raw column names + internal carrier columns (scan_id, mzs, full sequence, ...) instead of the oracle Tabulator curated titles / fixed+placeholder(-1->'-') formatters / per-table initial sort / hidden internals. findings.desc: series_column=isotope merges two same-(charge,isotope) traces the oracle keeps separate (per-trace -1000 breaks); 22/1437 feature groups affected. Mint per-trace id (trace_in_feature) + series_column=trace_in_feature
proteins frame lacks an is_best_per_scan flag needed for the ProteinTable best-per-spectrum default view

tables omit go_to_fields -> auto-detect exposes internal carriers (index/protein_id/tag_id/mzs); oracle curated lists (scan ['id','Scan']; protein ['Scan','accession']; tag ['Scan','StartPos','EndPos','TagSequence'])
fdr/id_fdr title 'Score Distribution' vs oracle 'FDR Plot'
density trace legend 'QScore (Target/Decoy)' vs oracle 'Target/Decoy QScores'
3D static title 'Precursor Signals' vs oracle dynamic 'Precursor signals'/'Mass signals' (populated state = 'Mass signals')
quant feature table title 'Features' vs oracle 'Feature groups'

anno_spectrum static is_signal highlight (all signal peaks orange) vs oracle selection-driven highlight of the SELECTED mass's m/z peaks + per-charge z-labels + mode-bar toggles; PLAN-SANCTIONED generalization (PHASE3_PLAN 495-498)
ProteinTable best-per-spectrum default-ON filter (max-Score per Scan) + toggle absent; migrated shows all proteoforms
protein/tag go_to_fields expose internal carriers vs oracle curated Scan-first lists
